nodes.label: AI as supplementary discovery tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as primary or sole source
nodes.grounding_span: I initially did my own traditional approach for finding papers, and found several of interest, but then also used AI after to try and see if there were papers I missed

nodes.label: AI as initial summarizer for triage
nodes.label_negative: AI as final authority on content
nodes.grounding_span: I sometimes used AI as an initial tool to get a quick summary to see if the paper was actually of interest

nodes.label: AI as verification and comparison partner
nodes.grounding_span: I would sometimes compare my own summaries to AI summaries to see these differences

nodes.label: AI as calculation assistant with stepwise validation
nodes.grounding_span: For the calculations, we validated each step by checking each step to make sure it made sense

nodes.label: AI as data analysis and debugging aid
nodes.grounding_span: AI can sometimes help with debugging / checking for errors, but I find AI still doesn't have a super comprehensive checking

nodes.label: AI as powerful but exploitable tool in science
nodes.label_negative: AI as safe and fully controllable tool
nodes.grounding_span: it's obviously an incredibly powerful tool, and can be used for a lot of good, but can also be exploited very easily

nodes.label: Epistemic integrity and personal understanding
nodes.grounding_span: my final lit review was entirely my own work and my own summarizing

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: to be able to say what my goal output is and what I'm trying to create, and have AI help give me the steps to do in R

nodes.label: Societal protection and responsible AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I think there need to be a lot more safeguards in place that can help protect us as a society from over-exploitation of AI

nodes.label: Cautious openness to AI as supplementary tool
nodes.grounding_span: I used AI in a few parts: first, in the literature review, used AI (of different forms) to help identify papers of interest

nodes.label: Skepticism about AI's current data analysis capability
nodes.grounding_span: AI still doesn't have a super comprehensive checking, and often isn't as helpful as I want

nodes.label: Fear and hesitation about future AI exploitation
nodes.grounding_span: I am honestly scared and hesitant about a lot of the use of AI in the future

nodes.label: Iterative validation and cross-checking
nodes.grounding_span: I almost always double-check all AI outputs in some way or another

nodes.label: Comparative reasoning between own and AI outputs
nodes.grounding_span: I would sometimes compare my own summaries to AI summaries to see these differences
